title: asplenium adiantum-nigrum subsp. centovallense
intro: Asplenium adiantum-nigrum subsp. centovallense, also known as Centovallian maidenhair spleenwort, is a rare subspecies of the Asplenium adiantum-nigrum fern species. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Centovallian maidenhair spleenwort have been reported to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exerting antimicrobial effects. Centovallian maidenhair spleenwort can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is typically consumed after being dried and powdered.
